extraction.one_line_summary: HERSHEY Marc won stage 4 sprint from elite breakaway after repeatedly splitting peloton on climbs, defeating WELLENS Tim by inches.
tactical_patterns: DEPLUS Laurens (Jumbo-Visma) repeatedly attacked on climbs forcing selection, but teammates in chase neutralized advantage showing conflicting team interests.
20-year-old HERSHEY refused to work final kilometers despite being weakest sprinter, stared down DEPLUS demanding he pace—youth boldness won.
GARCIA Ivan bridged to leaders but was deliberately dropped on final climb as trio recognized sprint danger, textbook pre-finish elimination.
WELLENS Tim went early knowing uphill drag finish favored sitting in, but HERSHEY's tactical rest in slipstream gave winning acceleration.

rider_signals.HERSHEY Marc: positive: 20-year-old U23 champion showed tactical maturity refusing to work despite being outnumbered, won uphill sprint against experienced pros
rider_signals.DEPLUS Laurens: negative: attacked multiple times to split race but couldn't shake companions when it mattered, forced to work despite being strongest climber
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: experienced enough to lead out early on uphill finish but lost by inches, knows course from previous win yet miscalculated final effort
rider_signals.GARCIA Ivan: negative: bridged heroically to leaders but was isolated and deliberately dropped on final climb, marking him as dangerous sprinter to eliminate
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: reached for rain cape early signaling acceptance he'd finish behind winners, psychological concession on tough course
